intro.title: Introduction to Data Structures
intro.content:
[b]What Is Data?[/b]
[i]Data[/i] refers to raw facts and values that represent information.
These can be numbers, words, symbols, or even measurements.

Examples of data:
-A student’s age: 18
-A name: "Juan"
-A test score: 95
-A yes/no value: true

By itself, data does not always make sense. It becomes useful only when it is organized, processed, and used correctly.

[b]Why Do We Need to Organize Data?[/b]
In computer programs, data is everywhere.

Programs deal with:
-Student records
-Game scores
-Messages
-Bank transactions
-Sensor readings

If data is not organized properly:
-Programs become slow
-Code becomes hard to understand
-Errors become more likely

To solve these problems, computer scientists use data structures.

[b]What Is a Data Structure?[/b]
A [i]data structure[/i] is a way of storing and organizing data in a computer so that it can be used efficiently.
It defines:
-How data is stored in memory
-How data can be accessed
-How data can be updated or removed
Different problems require different ways of organizing data.
There is no single “best” data structure for all situations.

[b]Real-Life Analogy[/b]
Think of data like books.
-If books are piled randomly on the floor, it is hard to find one.
-If books are arranged on shelves by category, finding a book is easier.
A data structure is like the shelf system that helps you manage the books properly.

[b]Importance of Data Structures[/b]
Data structures help programmers:
-Write faster programs
-Use memory efficiently
-Manage large amounts of data
-Solve complex problems logically

Many important applications depend on data structures, such as:
-Search engines
-Social media platforms
-Navigation systems
-Databases
-Games

[b]Data Structures in Programming[/b]
In programming, data structures are used to:
-Store collections of data
-Control how data flows in a program
-Represent real-world situations
Choosing the right data structure can greatly improve a program’s performance and readability.

[b]What You Will Learn Next[/b]
In the next lessons, you will study specific types of data structures, including:
-Array
-Linked List
-Stack
-Queue
-Tree
-Binary Tree
-Binary Search Tree
-Graph
Each of these has its own purpose and use cases.

[b]Summary[/b]
-Data is raw information.
-Data structures organize data efficiently.
-Good data organization makes programs faster and easier to maintain.
-Different problems require different data structures.
Understanding data structures is a fundamental skill in computer science and programming.
